summary: Changed the repository clang-format configuration from `SortIncludes: Never` to `SortIncludes: CaseInsensitive` and applied the resulting include-order churn across 138 files. The PR is a broad formatting/cleanup change affecting headers and C sources in Dolphin, sysdolphin baselib, fighter code, stage/game-mode code, and other melee-core areas. No functional code changes are evident from the provided diff excerpt; the edits are include reordering only.
what_changed: Existing include blocks were reformatted to match clang-format's case-insensitive include sorting.
The change touched 138 files with 275 added and 286 deleted lines, indicating large mechanical include-order churn rather than substantive implementation changes.

Examples from the diff show `<dolphin/gx/types.h>` being sorted before `<dolphin/types.h>`, `<sysdolphin/baselib/objalloc.h>` before `<sysdolphin/baselib/object.h>`, and headers such as `__GXInit.h`/`__GX_unknown_001.h` moved according to case-insensitive lexical order.
Representative affected areas include Dolphin OS/GX/CARD/AX/MTX/DSP files, sysdolphin baselib headers and sources, and many fighter character files.
smart_moves: Made the include-order rule explicit in `.clang-format` instead of relying on contributor-specific manual ordering.
Applied the formatting rule repository-wide in the same PR, preventing future developers from seeing unrelated include-sort churn after enabling the option locally.

Used `CaseInsensitive`, which is important in this codebase because include paths mix lowercase project directories with uppercase SDK-style names such as `GXInit`, `OSInit`, `CARDMount`, and `Runtime/__mem.h`.
Kept the change mechanical and narrow: the visible diff only reorders includes, which lowers risk for matching and behavior.
decomp_lessons: Formatting configuration changes should be isolated from decompilation/matching changes because include reordering can touch many files and obscure semantic diffs.
For this repository, future source additions should expect clang-format to sort includes case-insensitively.
When adding or moving headers with mixed-case Dolphin SDK names, run clang-format before review to avoid noisy include-order comments or follow-up churn.

Mechanical include sorting may change textual order without changing dependencies; if a file requires a nonstandard include order for compilation, that should be handled explicitly rather than relying on unsorted includes.
Large tree-wide cleanup PRs are acceptable when the rule being applied is clear and encoded in tooling.
naming_conventions: Include paths retain existing project naming conventions: lowercase project paths such as `<melee/ft/...>` and `<sysdolphin/baselib/...>`, mixed with SDK-style uppercase names such as `<dolphin/gx/GXInit.h>`, `<dolphin/os/OSInterrupt.h>`, and `<Runtime/__mem.h>`.
The PR does not introduce new symbol, function, struct, or file naming conventions; it only enforces include sorting.
Case-insensitive sorting means uppercase SDK component names should not be manually grouped ahead of or behind lowercase names unless separated by blank lines or otherwise exempted.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly-level matching tactic is evidenced in this PR.
The useful matching-adjacent tactic is process-oriented: keep clang-format include sorting separate from function matching work so generated diffs remain reviewable.

Because includes can affect declarations and macros, any future use of sorted includes should still be validated by the normal build/match checks, but this PR provides no evidence of matching-specific fixes.
review_feedback: No issue comments or review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The PR had one review and was merged quickly, but the slice does not include reviewer text or rationale.
follow_up_queries: Find PRs after 660 that touch `.clang-format` or include sorting policy.
Find build failures or follow-up fixes caused by clang-format include sorting.
Search for files with comments or manual blank-line grouping around includes that may intentionally resist automatic sorting.
Compare later decomp PRs for whether they separate formatting-only include churn from matching changes.
